data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but never added to the compilation. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file but doesn't include it in the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Add a `<Compile Include>` item for the generated file in the target.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates `GeneratedInfo.cs` before `CoreCompile`, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild needs to know about the file via `<Compile Include="..." />` for it to be passed to the C# compiler.

The fix is to add a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` item after writing the file in the target.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />"}

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it. Adding `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target adds the generated file to the compilation item group so the C# compiler picks it up.
